content: {"analyses": [{"source_url": "https://www.researchgate.net/publication/358336947_The_Impact_of_the_Lamination_Process_on_the_Adhesion_Properties_at_the_Glass-Encapsulant_Interface_and_Damp_Heat_Stability_of_PV_Modules", "source_title": "The Impact of the Lamination Process on the Adhesion Properties at the Glass-Encapsulant Interface and Damp Heat Stability of PV Modules", "source_type": "journal_article", "source_quality": 0.0, "overall_relevance": 0.0, "year": 0, "authors": [], "venue": "", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [], "evidence_summary": "INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT: ResearchGate returned empty content body despite highly relevant title. No article text available for extraction."}, {"source_url": "https://www.mdpi.com/2073-4360/14/9/1721", "source_title": "Influence of Hydrothermal Aging under Two Typical Adhesives on the Mechanical Properties of BFRP-BFRP Joints", "source_type": "journal_article", "source_quality": 0.73, "overall_relevance": 0.58, "year": 2022, "authors": [], "venue": "Polymers (MDPI)", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "Under damp heat aging at 80 degrees Celsius and 95% relative humidity, the saturated water absorption content of Araldite 2012 epoxy adhesive reached approximately 14.05% while Araldite 2015 adhesive reached approximately 16.03%, following Fick's law diffusion behavior with initial linear absorption followed by saturation plateau.", "direct_quote": "the saturated water absorption content of A2012 adhesive is about 14.05%, and the saturated water absorption content of A2015 adhesive is about 16.03%. Generally speaking, part of the water absorbed in the first stage enters the adhesive as free water, occupying a certain space, mainly filling pores and cavities, and supplemented by the reaction of adsorbed water molecules with some hydrophilic functional groups. This leads to plasticization of the adhesive", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.62, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["Araldite 2012", "14.05%", "Araldite 2015", "16.03%", "80°C", "95% RH", "Fick's law", "water absorption"]}, {"statement": "High temperature and high humidity combined cause accelerated joint deterioration through the damp heat aging effect, where elevated temperature increases both the chemical reaction rate and the diffusion coefficient, promoting more water diffusion that causes aging and degradation affecting mechanical properties of bonded joints.", "direct_quote": "Temperature and humidity have two different effects on adhesive joints. When the two conditions work together, the adhesive joint will further deteriorate. This phenomenon is called the damp heat aging effect. In the case of high temperature and high humidity, a higher temperature will increase the rate of the chemical reaction and the diffusion coefficient, promote more water diffusion, and further cause aging and degradation of the joint, thereby affecting the mechanical properties", "fact_category": "causal_link", "relevance_score": 0.55, "confidence": 0.85, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["damp heat aging effect", "diffusion coefficient", "chemical reaction rate", "mechanical properties", "adhesive joints"]}, {"statement": "Liu et al. reported that tensile strength of high-temperature curing epoxy adhesive joints decreased by approximately 31.3% after moisture absorption alone, 27.7% under high temperature (95 degrees Celsius) dry conditions, and 54.4% under combined high temperature and humid conditions, demonstrating synergistic degradation.", "direct_quote": "Liu et al. studied the effect of high temperature curing epoxy adhesive on the tensile strength of joints under different aging environments. It was observed that the tensile strength decreased by about 31.3% after moisture absorption, and decreased by 27.7% and 54.4% under high temperature (95 °C)/dry environment and high temperature (95 °C)/humid environment, respectively. Zhong et al. comparatively studied the damp-heat durability of glass and carbon fiber reinforced composites", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.52, "confidence": 0.82, "perspective": "Methodological", "entities": ["tensile strength", "31.3%", "27.7%", "54.4%", "95°C", "moisture absorption", "epoxy adhesive"]}], "evidence_summary": "MDPI study on hydrothermal aging of BFRP composite adhesive joints using Araldite epoxy adhesives at 80°C/95% RH. Key data: saturated water absorption 14-16%, tensile strength losses 27-54% under combined damp-heat. Relevant to understanding moisture diffusion and adhesion degradation mechanisms applicable to encapsulant systems."}]}
